packages.name: asn1-rs
packages.description: Parser/encoder for ASN.1 BER/DER data

packages.name: asn1-rs-derive
packages.description: Derive macros for the `asn1-rs` crate

packages.name: asn1-rs-impl
packages.description: Implementation details for the `asn1-rs` crate

packages.name: autocfg
packages.description: Automatic cfg for Rust compiler features

packages.name: base64
packages.description: encodes and decodes base64 as bytes or utf8

packages.name: base64ct
packages.description:
Pure Rust implementation of Base64 (RFC 4648) which avoids any usages of
data-dependent branches/LUTs and thereby provides portable "best effort"
constant-time operation and embedded-friendly no_std support

packages.name: block-buffer
packages.description: Buffer type for block processing of data

packages.name: cfg-if
packages.description:
A macro to ergonomically define an item depending on a large number of #[cfg]
parameters. Structured like an if-else chain, the first matching branch is the
item that gets emitted.

packages.name: const-oid
packages.description:
Const-friendly implementation of the ISO/IEC Object Identifier (OID) standard
as defined in ITU X.660, with support for BER/DER encoding/decoding as well as
heapless no_std (i.e. embedded) support

packages.name: cpufeatures
packages.description:
Lightweight runtime CPU feature detection for aarch64, loongarch64, and x86/x86_64 targets, 
with no_std support and support for mobile targets including Android and iOS

packages.name: crypto-common
packages.description: Common cryptographic traits

packages.name: curve25519-dalek
packages.description: A pure-Rust implementation of group operations on ristretto255 and Curve25519

packages.name: curve25519-dalek-derive
packages.description: curve25519-dalek Derives

packages.name: data-encoding
packages.description: Efficient and customizable data-encoding functions like base64, base32, and hex

packages.name: der
packages.description:
Pure Rust embedded-friendly implementation of the Distinguished Encoding Rules
(DER) for Abstract Syntax Notation One (ASN.1) as described in ITU X.690 with
full support for heapless no_std targets

packages.name: der-parser
packages.description: Parser/encoder for ASN.1 BER/DER data

packages.name: deranged
packages.description: Ranged integers

packages.name: digest
packages.description: Traits for cryptographic hash functions and message authentication codes

packages.name: displaydoc
packages.description: A derive macro for implementing the display Trait via a doc comment and string interpolation

packages.name: ed25519
packages.description:
Edwards Digital Signature Algorithm (EdDSA) over Curve25519 (as specified in RFC 8032)
support library providing signature type definitions and PKCS#8 private key
decoding/encoding support

packages.name: ed25519-dalek
packages.description: Fast and efficient ed25519 EdDSA key generations, signing, and verification in pure Rust.

packages.name: fiat-crypto
packages.description: Fiat-crypto generated Rust

packages.name: generic-array
packages.description: Generic types implementing functionality of arrays

packages.name: getrandom
packages.description: A small cross-platform library for retrieving random data from system source

packages.name: itoa
packages.description: Fast integer primitive to string conversion

packages.name: lazy_static
packages.description: A macro for declaring lazily evaluated statics in Rust.

packages.name: libc
packages.description: Raw FFI bindings to platform libraries like libc.

packages.name: memchr
packages.description:
Provides extremely fast (uses SIMD on x86_64, aarch64 and wasm32) routines for
1, 2 or 3 byte search and single substring search.

packages.name: minimal-lexical
packages.description: Fast float parsing conversion routines.

packages.name: nom
packages.description: A byte-oriented, zero-copy, parser combinators library

packages.name: num-bigint
packages.description: Big integer implementation for Rust

packages.name: num-conv
packages.description:
`num_conv` is a crate to convert between integer types without using `as` casts. This provides
better certainty when refactoring, makes the exact behavior of code more explicit, and allows using
turbofish syntax.

packages.name: num-integer
packages.description: Integer traits and functions

packages.name: num-traits
packages.description: Numeric traits for generic mathematics

packages.name: oid-registry
packages.description: Object Identifier (OID) database

packages.name: pkcs8
packages.description:
Pure Rust implementation of Public-Key Cryptography Standards (PKCS) #8:
Private-Key Information Syntax Specification (RFC 5208), with additional
support for PKCS#8v2 asymmetric key packages (RFC 5958)

packages.name: powerfmt
packages.description:
`powerfmt` is a library that provides utilities for formatting values. This crate makes it
    significantly easier to support filling to a minimum width with alignment, avoid heap
    allocation, and avoid repetitive calculations.

packages.name: ppv-lite86
packages.description: Cross-platform cryptography-oriented low-level SIMD library.

packages.name: proc-macro2
packages.description: A substitute implementation of the compiler's `proc_macro` API to decouple token-based libraries from the procedural macro use case.

packages.name: quote
packages.description: Quasi-quoting macro quote!(...)

packages.name: rand
packages.description: Random number generators and other randomness functionality.

packages.name: rand_chacha
packages.description: ChaCha random number generator

packages.name: rand_core
packages.description: Core random number generator traits and tools for implementation.

packages.name: rustc_version
packages.description: A library for querying the version of a installed rustc compiler

packages.name: rusticata-macros
packages.description: Helper macros for Rusticata

packages.name: semver
packages.description: Parser and evaluator for Cargo's flavor of Semantic Versioning

packages.name: serde
packages.description: A generic serialization/deserialization framework

packages.name: serde_core
packages.description: Serde traits only, with no support for derive -- use the `serde` crate instead

packages.name: serde_derive
packages.description: Macros 1.1 implementation of #[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]

packages.name: sha2
packages.description:
Pure Rust implementation of the SHA-2 hash function family
including SHA-224, SHA-256, SHA-384, and SHA-512.

packages.name: signature
packages.description: Traits for cryptographic signature algorithms (e.g. ECDSA, Ed25519)

packages.name: spki
packages.description:
X.509 Subject Public Key Info (RFC5280) describing public keys as well as their
associated AlgorithmIdentifiers (i.e. OIDs)

packages.name: subtle
packages.description: Pure-Rust traits and utilities for constant-time cryptographic implementations.

packages.name: syn
packages.description: Parser for Rust source code

packages.name: synstructure
packages.description: Helper methods and macros for custom derives

packages.name: thiserror
packages.description: derive(Error)

packages.name: thiserror-impl
packages.description: Implementation detail of the `thiserror` crate

packages.name: time
packages.description: Date and time library. Fully interoperable with the standard library. Mostly compatible with #![no_std].

packages.name: time-core
packages.description: This crate is an implementation detail and should not be relied upon directly.

packages.name: time-macros
packages.description:
Procedural macros for the time crate.
    This crate is an implementation detail and should not be relied upon directly.

packages.name: typenum
packages.description:
Typenum is a Rust library for type-level numbers evaluated at
    compile time. It currently supports bits, unsigned integers, and signed
    integers. It also provides a type-level array of type-level numbers, but its
    implementation is incomplete.

packages.name: unicode-ident
packages.description: Determine whether characters have the XID_Start or XID_Continue properties according to Unicode Standard Annex #31

packages.name: version_check
packages.description: Tiny crate to check the version of the installed/running rustc.

packages.name: wasi
packages.license: Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception OR Apache-2.0 OR MIT
packages.description: Experimental WASI API bindings for Rust

packages.name: x509-parser
packages.description: Parser for the X.509 v3 format (RFC 5280 certificates)

packages.name: zerocopy
packages.description: Zerocopy makes zero-cost memory manipulation effortless. We write "unsafe" so you don't have to.

packages.name: zerocopy-derive
packages.description: Custom derive for traits from the zerocopy crate

packages.name: zeroize
packages.description:
Securely clear secrets from memory with a simple trait built on
stable Rust primitives which guarantee memory is zeroed using an
operation will not be 'optimized away' by the compiler.
Uses a portable pure Rust implementation that works everywhere,
even WASM!
